shortAnswer: Vitamin B6 is involved in numerous metabolic processes, including nerve and protein metabolism. A deficiency can lead to fatigue, nerve problems, or skin changes, and occurs with an unbalanced diet or certain medications, among other causes.
metaDescription: Vitamin B6 too low? Meaning, normal ranges, and causes of deficiency explained simply.
causesLow: Certain medications (e.g. some blood pressure or tuberculosis drugs)
doctorQuestions: Could my vitamin B6 deficiency explain my symptoms?
